focus: security: corpus-to-prompt and transport trust boundaries

findingsSummary.summary: Three P1 security planning-contract findings: missing hydration path containment, contradictory unknown-rights eligibility, and unspecified malicious-instruction handling for STUDY prompt material.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no path-containment or symlink safety contract
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact phase-004 path-safety search found no containment control; complete hydration and checklist sections were directly read.
findingDetails.claim: Manifest-selected artifact reads lack canonical-root and symlink containment requirements.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all phase-004 canonical documents for realpath, symlink, traversal, allowlist, relative-path, and outside-root controls; reviewed generation hashing.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Committed manifests and re-hashing reduce drift but do not prove resolved-path containment.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: An incorporated governing parser contract proves root containment and symlink rejection for every read.

findingDetails.title: Unknown-rights styles are both forbidden from results and permitted as decision references
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared eligibility ADR, P0 requirement, security NFR, edge case, and checklist across ranking, cards, hydration, and proof consumers.
findingDetails.claim: Rights-unknown styles are simultaneously excluded from results and eligible as rationale references.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed exact-reuse limitation and proof-card gate as possible narrower interpretations.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A reference-only state may be intended, but it is not typed and ADR-002 prohibits entry into results broadly.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Typed reference-only rights state, raw-hydration prohibition, reconciled ADR, and fixtures are specified.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope has no malicious-instruction handling or test oracle
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact phase-006 search found envelope/leak controls but no malicious, untrusted, sanitize, escape, or instruction-handling contract.
findingDetails.claim: STUDY does not define or test resistance to malicious corpus instructions.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed de-literalization, target-facts binding, malformed-envelope refusal, and all adversarial fixture requirements.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Those controls reduce copying and authority drift but do not define handling of semantically preserved instructions.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: The contract treats source text as inert data, neutralizes instruction-like content, and adds seeded fail-closed prompt-injection fixtures.

traceabilityChecks.note: Security contracts mapped; prior consumer route not retried.
Checklist lines used as contract evidence only; blocked completion-evidence protocol not retried.

noveltyJustification: All three findings expose security contract classes not present in iteration 1; weightedNew=15 and weightedTotal=25, so ratio=0.60.
edgeCases: Findings concern planned contracts, not active runtime exploits.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Complex phase-parent security scope with seven planned children and named runtime integration surfaces.

selectedAreas: phase-004 retrieval and hydration trust boundary
phases 007-010 shared seam, mode, and transport contracts
targetSelection.selectionRationale: Rendered security focus required path, provenance/rights, injection, leakage, staleness, no-cache, and fail-open/fail-closed review.

limitations: No runtime exploit test because phases 004-010 are planned scaffolds

searchLedger.scope: externalized state, strategy, config, registry, and shared severity doctrine
searchLedger.result: Confirmed iteration 2 security focus, prior findings, blocked approaches, lineage, and P1 doctrine.
Mapped path, injection, rights, leakage, generation, no-cache, and fallback claims; code graph remained absent.

searchLedger.scope: phase 004 retrieval spec, ADRs, and checklist
searchLedger.result: Confirmed missing path containment and contradictory unknown-rights eligibility with counterevidence.

searchLedger.scope: phase 006 STUDY spec/checklist and phases 007-010 security contracts
searchLedger.result: Confirmed prompt-injection oracle gap; ruled out stale-generation and no-cache duplicate findings.

title: Hydration has no path-containment or symlink safety contract

counterevidence: Manifest commitment, selected-artifact re-hash, and generation mismatch reduce drift but do not establish root containment.

title: Unknown-rights styles are both forbidden from results and permitted as decision references

counterevidence: Exact-reuse prohibition may imply a narrower reference-only state, but that state is not typed and the ADR forbids rights-unknown styles from results broadly.

title: The STUDY injection envelope has no malicious-instruction handling or test oracle

counterevidence: De-literalization, locked FACTS, and malformed-envelope refusal reduce copying and authority drift but do not define malicious-instruction handling.

reason: Rendered dispatch declared code graph absent; v2 graphless direct-read and exact-search ledger used.
No security-sensitive runtime exists yet and no active exploit path was evidenced.
Canonical phase-010 documents consistently prohibit raw corpus/Open-Design payload caching; implementation verification remains future work.
